name: The route of the Garbí chains - Circular from Segart
name_original: La ruta de las cadenas del Garbí — Circular desde Segart
region_intro: Hiking around Segart offers access to the mountainous terrain of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, characterized by its diverse flora including pine forests and Mediterranean shrubs. The region features distinctive geological formations, with mountains often topped with crags and cliffs. Prominent peaks such as Mount Garbí and Mola de Segart provide panoramic views and challenging ascents. The landscape is shaped by both Buntsandstein and Muschelkalt geological structures, contributing to its unique relief. Best hiking trails around Segart The most popular hiking route is The route of the Garbí chains - Circular from Segart, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area around Mount Garbí. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Xocainet – Serra Calderona Natural Park loop from Segart, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This trail leads through the varied landscapes of the natural park. Local hikers also love the Mola de Segart – La Mola de Segart loop from Segart, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain to the distinctive Mola de Segart, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Segart is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and prominent crags and cliffs, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Segart are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Segart's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Segart?
A: There are 14 hiking trails around Segart. These routes offer a variety of experiences within the mountainous terrain of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Segart?
A: Hikes in Segart feature mountainous terrain with prominent crags and cliffs, often oriented to the South, Southeast, and East. You'll encounter pine forests, Mediterranean shrubs, and distinctive geological formations like Buntsandstein and Muschelkalt, especially around peaks like Mount Garbí and Mola de Segart.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Segart?
A: Yes, Segart is known for its challenging routes. For experienced trekkers, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e816845654/the-route-of-the-garbi-chains-circular-from-segart" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The route of the Garbí chains - Circular from Segart&lt;/a&gt; (difficult, 7.3 km) or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14710994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segart – Canal de Garbí Scramble loop from Segart&lt;/a&gt; (difficult, 4.0 km) offer rewarding ascents and rugged paths.

Q: Can I find family-friendly hiking options in Segart?
A: While many routes in Segart are rated difficult, there are moderate options that might be suitable for families with some hiking experience. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21958530" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Xocainet – Serra Calderona Natural Park loop from Segart&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.2 km path that explores varied landscapes. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Segart?
A: Segart is located within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within natural parks, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check specific park regulations before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Segart?
A: Yes, many of the popular hikes around Segart are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e816845654/the-route-of-the-garbi-chains-circular-from-segart" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The route of the Garbí chains - Circular from Segart&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3962110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola de Segart – La Mola de Segart loop from Segart&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the notable natural landmarks or viewpoints I can see while hiking?
A: Segart offers several prominent natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can hike to &lt;strong&gt;Mount Garbí&lt;/strong&gt; (600m) for the well-known Mirador de Garbí viewpoint, or ascend &lt;strong&gt;Mola de Segart&lt;/strong&gt; (575m), recognizable by its peculiar 'millstone' shape, which provides panoramic views extending to the Sierra de Espadán and Peñagolosa.

Q: Are there historical sites or castles to explore near Segart?
A: Yes, the region around Segart has several historical sites. You can explore nearby castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Serra&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3954829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sagunto Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4091139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torres Torres Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Segart itself also has the ruins of a 12th-century castle and an 18th-century church.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Segart?
A: The Sierra Calderona Natural Park, where Segart is located, is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter offers cooler temperatures but can sometimes bring unpredictable weather.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Segart?
A: Segart is a small village, and parking is typically available within or on the outskirts of the village, often near the starting points of popular trails. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, as spaces can be limited.

Q: Is it possible to reach Segart by public transport for hiking?
A: Public transport options to Segart are limited. It is generally more convenient to reach Segart by car. For detailed information on bus routes or other transport, it's best to consult local transport authorities or check services from larger nearby towns like Sagunto or Valencia.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Segart?
A: The trails in Segart are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Garbí and Mola de Segart, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the natural beauty of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: Are there any gorges or canyons to explore near Segart?
A: Yes, the area around Segart features interesting geological formations including barrancos (ravines) and gorges. You can explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail Along Barranco Codobal&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2785413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PortaCeli Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which offer unique hiking experiences through rugged landscapes.
tips: The "Canal de Garbí" is a section of some technical difficulty, and offers one of the best views of the Serra Calderona and the Gulf of Valencia.
Typical mountain of the region, the Garbí, has several different climbs and can be accessed by road, road, path or even climbing.
